title: Water: CABIN - Canada.ca
content:
## Canadian Aquatic Biomonitoring Network

## Canadian Aquatic Biomonitoring Network
The Canadian Aquatic Biomonitoring Network (CABIN) is a collaborative network, managed by ECCC, for the biomonitoring of freshwater ecosystem health using standardized protocols and web accessible tools.
- CABIN field techniques video

## Services and information
Overview of what CABIN is, why biomonitoring is used, how CABIN is managed.
Learn about the benefits of CABIN and advantages of using benthic macroinvertebrates.
Look for data on the Open Government portal or if you are a network participant, log in to the CABIN database.
Locate CABIN sampling sites across the country using the Open Maps Viewer.

### Training and certification
Nationally-standardized training for professionals and citizens. Locate your closest field course.
Resources developed by ECCC to assist network participants in the application of CABIN projects (field techniques video, manuals, field sheets, checklists and quick reference guides).
Resources relevant to the biological monitoring produced by CABIN partners (CABIN related research papers, reports, videos, links).

### News, updates and events
From program updates to upcoming events, here you can stay informed.
- Facebook: Environment and Natural Resources in Canada
- Environment and Climate Change Canada

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
